admission_rule.state_space: countable effectively presented state and outcome carriers
admission_rule.kernels: computable probability kernels with certified effective tail bounds or computable summation moduli
admission_rule.queries: finite rational-cylinder events, bounded computable observables, and finite stopping-horizon questions
admission_rule.conditioning: conditioning only on events with certified positive probability and an effective denominator bound
admission_rule.reasoning_layer: finite commitments, grounds, dependencies, revisions, evidential annotations, and declared probabilistic updates
admission_rule.presentation: uniform algorithms for point masses, cylinder probabilities, tail certificates, expectation approximants, and update certificates
exclusions: uncountable carriers without an effective presentation and computable integration interface
conditioning on null or effectively undecidable zero-probability events
unbounded observables without certified integrability moduli
infinite-horizon claims without effective convergence certificates

observation_boundary.analyst_access: full frozen effective presentation and certificates
observation_boundary.represented_agent_access: only source-declared observations, beliefs, commitments, and history
prohibited: finite support truncation treated as the exact distribution

nonclaims: all stochastic reasoning systems are admitted
finite truncations are exact infinite-support distributions
conditioning on arbitrary null events is defined
stochastic representation establishes a universal structure
necessity minimality or uniqueness is established
